problems: Scaffolding rejection rate is 1300.0% (13 rejections) — model output failing content guards
plan:
Using `mira-general-what-so-what-now-what` for structure: facts, implication, action.

**Adversarial Checks**

DISSENT CHECK: No score above `0.5` is assigned here, so no falsification check is required.

INVERSION CHECK: No entry scored `<= 0.3` uses positive language such as “good,” “strong,” “accurate,” or “improved.” No inverted entries.

**Concrete Action Items**

| Diagnostic insight | Agent / owner | Specific action | Expected impact | Verification |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| Scaffolding rejection rate is reported as `1300.0%`, which is mathematically suspicious for a rate | Metrics owner for scaffolding | Fix the rejection-rate metric to emit numerator, denominator, and time window: `rejections / scaffold_attempts`. Add a guardrail that flags any rate above `100%` as invalid telemetry. | Prevents misleading severity reports and separates real agent failure from metric bugs. | Dashboard shows `13 rejections / N attempts`; no percent metric can exceed `100%`. |
| 13 scaffolding rejections indicate guard failures but not why | Scaffolding agent | Log every rejection with `guard_rule_id`, `rejection_category`, `input_task_type`, `model_output_hash`, and `retry_count`. | Makes the rejection pattern diagnosable instead of a flat failure count. | Weekly report groups rejections by rule; top 3 rejection causes are visible. |
| Scaffolding output is failing deterministic content guards | Scaffolding agent | Add a pre-output validator before final emission that checks forbidden patterns: unsafe network calls, credential access, destructive filesystem commands, privilege escalation, obfuscated payloads, executable code where not requested, and invalid scaffold structure. | Catches bad outputs before they hit production guards. | Guard rejections drop from `13/week` to `<=2/week`; validator catches produce structured reasons. |
| Generated/imported skills require security audit before save/enable | Skill/scaffolding agent | Add a mandatory `security_audit_skill(skill_text)` step before writing or enabling any `.md` skill. Block suspicious network requests, filesystem deletion, code execution, credential reads, obfuscation, and privilege escalation. | Enforces the workspace hard rule and moves failures earlier. | Add fixtures: benign skill passes; exfiltration, deletion, obfuscation, credential, and privilege fixtures fail. |
| Scaffolding prompt likely permits unsafe or overbroad output | Scaffolding agent | Update the scaffolding system prompt in the owning `handler.py` to require guard-safe output: no credential access, no destructive commands, no hidden execution, no network behavior unless explicitly requested, and all generated skills must pass audit before enablement. | Reduces model-generated guard violations at the source. | A/B compare rejection categories before and after prompt change. |
| Podcast pipeline has 50 failures in 7 days but no weak-agent attribution | Podcast pipeline orchestrator | Add per-stage attribution fields to every job: `stage`, `agent_name`, `handler`, `input_id`, `artifact_path`, `exception_type`, `retryable`, `duration_ms`. | Identifies whether failures come from ingest, transcript, segmentation, writing, audio processing, export, or publish. | Within 48 hours, `95%+` of failures have stage and agent attribution. |
| Podcast failures may be malformed input rather than model failure | Podcast pipeline orchestrator | Add preflight validation before agent dispatch: non-empty audio/transcript, supported codec, duration bounds, readable encoding, required metadata, artifact path writable, transcript language detected. | Reduces avoidable downstream failures. | Downstream failures fall while `preflight_rejected_count` becomes explainable by rule. |
| Podcast pipeline may be retrying the wrong failures or not retrying transient ones | Podcast pipeline orchestrator | Add bounded retries only for retryable classes: model/API timeout, temporary file lock, export race, transient network error. Use max `2` retries with exponential backoff; never retry deterministic validation failures. | Recovers transient failures without hiding broken inputs. | Track `retry_success_rate`, `repeat_failure_by_stage`, and final failure rate. |
| 50 failures in 7 days indicates missing operational alerting | Podcast pipeline owner | Add alerts for `failure_rate > baseline + threshold`, `same_stage_failures >= 5/day`, and `unclassified_failures > 5%`. Page only on production-blocking stages; ticket otherwise. | Shortens detection time and prevents silent accumulation. | Alert fires in a synthetic failure test and includes stage, job, and artifact links. |
| Confidence calibration was not scored because no relevant outputs were sampled | Evaluation owner | Add a weekly calibration eval for podcast notes, summaries, intros, and any analyst/researcher/writer outputs. Count unsupported empirical claims and missing uncertainty markers. | Prevents overconfident generated content from passing unnoticed. | Sample 20 outputs/week; target zero unsupported factual assertions and explicit markers for uncertain claims. |
| Podcast writer/editor agents may generate unverifiable claims | Podcast writer/editor agents | Add prompt rule: empirical claims require source attribution or retrieval; uncertain claims must use markers such as “likely,” “uncertain,” “not verified,” or “I don’t have enough signal.” | Reduces overconfident or unsupported content in published materials. | Manual audit finds no unsupported factual claims in sampled episode notes. |
